title: Annihilation
logline: A biologist joins an expedition into a mysterious environmental anomaly called Area X to find her missing husband, only to discover a reality-altering force that transforms everything it touches.
antagonistOrConflict: The Shimmer/Area X - an alien force that refracts and transforms DNA, creating duplicates and mutations
tone: Cerebral, atmospheric, and increasingly unsettling with body horror elements
themes: The nature of change and transformation

actBreaks.description: The team enters Area X through the gates, transitioning from the interrogation/preparation phase to the expedition itself.

validation.ruling: The team's departure through the facility gates marks the irreversible transition from preparation to active mission, fundamentally changing the story's direction as they enter the unknown.

actBreaks.description: Lena leaves the decimated team behind in the statue garden and begins her solo journey to the lighthouse.

validation.ruling: Scene #103 is too brief and transitional. Scene #92 shows Lena's final moment of connection with her doppelganger before departing alone for the lighthouse, marking the crucial shift into the final act where she must face the source alone.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated triptych structure that mirrors its thematic concerns with transformation and identity. Part One (The Southern Reach) functions as an interrogation thriller, using the framing device of Lena's debriefing to create mystery and dread. Part Two (Area X) becomes a survival horror expedition film as the team encounters increasingly disturbing phenomena. Part Three (The Lighthouse) transforms into an intimate psychological confrontation with the alien force itself. The structure cleverly uses the interview scenes to create temporal displacement, allowing the audience to experience both the mystery of what happened and the horror of how it unfolds. Each section has its own distinct pacing and genre conventions while building toward the cosmic horror revelation.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates dramatically in the middle section with rapid scene cuts during the team's disintegration (scenes 44-103), then slows to an almost meditative pace for Lena's solo journey to the lighthouse. The final act uses extended sequences in the underground chamber to build maximum tension before the explosive climax.

incitingIncident.description: Kane sits at the kitchen table staring at water while Lena angrily demands explanations for his twelve-month disappearance, but he can only say the mission was covert and claims he doesn't remember where he was.

validation.ruling: Kane's mysterious return and inability to explain his year-long disappearance shatters Lena's ordinary world and establishes the central mystery that will drive her into the Shimmer to find answers.

midpoint.description: Lena wakes up in the abandoned base's rec room and sees Sheppard is also awake while Thorensen and Radek sleep nearby.

validation.ruling: Scene #64 is too passive as Lena simply wakes up. Scene #65 shows Dr. Ventress making the crucial decision to head to Ville Perdu instead of the lighthouse, fundamentally changing their mission direction and raising stakes after Sheppard's loss.

climax.description: Lena enters a chamber where Dr. Ventress stands glowing with cilia covering her skin, and Ventress transforms into a shimmering humanoid duplicate of Lena before the original Ventress disintegrates.

validation.ruling: Lena's confrontation with the transformed Dr. Ventress in the chamber represents the peak dramatic tension where she directly faces the alien entity and learns the truth about what's been transforming everyone in the Shimmer.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTER SPACE
scenes.summary: A meteor containing blue-green iridescence travels through space past the moon toward Earth.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTER ATMOSPHERE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The meteor burns in Earth's outer atmosphere and fragments apart.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER ATMOSPHERE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Most meteor fragments burn away except one piece that races toward the ocean near a lighthouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. LIGHTHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The meteor ember impacts near the lighthouse, drilling into the earth and revealing an alien creature with tendrils that begins dividing as a woman's voice mentions cells.
scenes.turningPointNote: The alien presence arrives on Earth and begins reproducing

scenes.heading: INT. JOHNS HOPKINS MEDICAL SCHOOL/SEMINAR ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena teaches medical students about cell division and cancer in a seminar room.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOHNS HOPKINS MEDICAL SCHOOL/CAMPUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Daniel approaches Lena on campus and invites her to a garden party, but she politely declines.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURB - DAY
scenes.summary: Children ride bikes down a suburban street in bright sunlight.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Inside a quiet house, photographs show Lena with her soldier husband Kane in various settings including their wedding and honeymoon.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE/LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena sits alone on her sofa holding an open locket with Kane's photo, struggling to control her breathing as tears spill down her face.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE/GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena searches through boxes in her garage and finds paint supplies.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE/BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena removes photographs of her and Kane from the walls and energetically paints over the bedroom walls while music plays.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE/KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kane sits at the kitchen table staring at water while Lena angrily demands explanations for his twelve-month disappearance, but he can only say the mission was covert and claims he doesn't remember where he was.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kane's mysterious return after a year sets the central mystery in motion

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kane vomits blood and convulses on a stretcher in an ambulance while a paramedic radios about his condition and Lena tries to comfort him.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The ambulance is flanked by three black SUVs and a helicopter shining a spotlight down at the windscreen.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The ambulance driver is blinded by the spotlight and slams on the brakes.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The ambulance skids on the street.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena and the paramedic are slammed against the ambulance wall while Kane remains strapped to the stretcher.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Armed spec ops soldiers surround the stopped ambulance, drag out Lena and the paramedic while Lena screams for them to let Kane go as a helicopter lands.
scenes.turningPointNote: Government forces capture Lena and Kane, escalating the situation dramatically

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/INTERVIEW ROOM
scenes.summary: Lena sits trembling in a windowless concrete room with dried blood on her clothes until Dr. Ventress enters offering water and introducing herself as a psychologist.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/SHOWER
scenes.summary: Lena showers off the blood in a military-style facility while a spec ops soldier guards the door.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/CORRIDOR
scenes.summary: Lena walks down a corridor with the guard and asks if he knows her husband Kane, noticing tranquilizer vials on his belt.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/INTERVIEW ROOM
scenes.summary: Lena pleads with the soldier for information about Kane, touching his chest emotionally while secretly stealing a tranquilizer and injecting him with it.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/CORRIDOR
scenes.summary: Lena bolts the interview room door shut and looks down the corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/CORRIDOR
scenes.summary: Lena moves quietly through the facility corridors in her sneakers.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/ELEVATOR LOBBY
scenes.summary: Lena discovers the elevator requires a biometric palm print just as it arrives with a chime.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/ELEVATOR
scenes.summary: Dr. Ventress argues with a spec ops captain in the elevator about her command authority and team structure.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/ELEVATOR LOBBY
scenes.summary: Lena hides against the wall as Dr. Ventress and the captain discuss Pentagon control taking over in forty-eight hours, then slips into the elevator.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/ELEVATOR
scenes.summary: Lena examines the elevator's four-level layout and discovers she needs biometric access when the buttons don't respond.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/MESS HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena enters a crowded mess hall and walks toward the only exit - glass doors leading to sunlight - while trying not to draw attention in her orange jumpsuit.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/VIEWING PLATFORM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena steps onto a viewing platform and stops, arrested by the view of a massive concrete wall with watchtowers and a shimmering distortion beyond it.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lena first sees the Shimmer and the scope of the containment operation

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/COMMAND CENTER - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Ventress explains in the command center that the Shimmer started three years ago around a lighthouse, showing Lena maps and photos of failed expedition teams.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/MEDICAL CENTRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena visits Kane in a freezing medical center where he lies on life support with ivory-colored skin and a heartbeat of four beats per minute.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/MEDICAL CENTRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Ventress tells Lena that Kane won't survive the facility's relocation and reveals she has terminal cancer and will lead a final mission to the lighthouse.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dr. Ventress recruits Lena for the final mission into the Shimmer

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/FIRING RANGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Three women - Anya Thorensen, Cass Sheppard, and Josie Radek - practice shooting automatic rifles at a firing range.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Southern Reach facility sits illuminated by floodlights under a starry night sky distorted by the Shimmer.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/VIEWING PLATFORM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena gazes at Kane's photo in her locket on the viewing platform while Thorensen approaches with binoculars and suggests they should bond as teammates.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SHIMMER/TREELINE - DAY
scenes.summary: Through binoculars, Thorensen shows Lena a bearded man in spec ops uniform walking out of the forest before being shot multiple times.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/VIEWING PLATFORM - DAY
scenes.summary: Thorensen suggests that Lieutenant Kane, the man in the freeze box, was the one who shot the soldier who emerged from the forest.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/GATES - DAWN
scenes.summary: The five women exit through the facility's massive metal gates at dawn, carrying backpacks and rifles as Dr. Ventress leads them into the deforested area.
scenes.turningPointNote: The expedition into the Shimmer begins

scenes.heading: EXT. DEFORESTED AREA - DAWN
scenes.summary: The team walks past a decomposing body with a bullet hole in the skull as they reach the treeline where Dr. Ventress calls for them to stay close.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SKY - DAY
scenes.summary: The full moon hangs in the clear blue daytime sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE/GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: In a flashback, Lena and Kane sunbathe in their garden discussing the daytime moon and cellular immortality, with Lena explaining how cells could theoretically avoid aging and death.

scenes.heading: INT. TENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena wakes with a jolt inside a bright orange tent, disoriented.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST CLEARING/CAMPSITE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena emerges from her tent to find a campsite in a forest clearing with her teammates, having no memory of how they got there, while the Shimmer distorts the sky above them.
scenes.turningPointNote: The team discovers they've lost time and memory after entering the Shimmer

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: The five women wade through knee-deep swamp water with heavy gear as Thorensen falls into a deeper hole and Lena helps pull her out.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP/FISHING HUT - DAY
scenes.summary: The team discovers a collapsed fishing hut covered in strangely varied flowers that Lena identifies as different species growing from the same plant structure.

scenes.heading: INT. GATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena peers inside the mouth of a dead alligator while the others hold its jaws open, discovering rows of shark-like teeth instead of normal alligator teeth.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: A swamp tree displays clusters of brightly colored moss growths in reds, purples, and rust colors that look diseased.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: The team travels in boats through the swamp as Lena notices a bruise on her forearm and lies to Sheppard about her husband being dead when asked about his military service.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: Thorensen spots something ahead as the boats approach a shoreline with a cluster of low concrete buildings.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED BASE - DAY
scenes.summary: The five women enter an abandoned military base with prefab buildings reclaimed by nature and covered in brightly-colored mosses, which Dr. Ventress identifies as mutations and the former Southern Reach headquarters.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED BASE/BARRACKS - DAY
scenes.summary: The team enters the barracks dormitory where Lena discovers someone's backpack on the ground near the beds.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED BASE/BARRACKS - DAY
scenes.summary: The five women gather around Radek's camera as she powers it up to view the footage.

scenes.heading: INT. SCREEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The camera screen shows Kane speaking directly to the cameraman, saying he doesn't know how long they've been there and has lost track of time.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED BASE/BARRACKS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena freezes upon seeing her husband Kane on the video while he continues speaking about losing track of time and everything else.

scenes.heading: INT. SCREEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kane on screen describes feeling like he's dreaming but knowing he can't be because he never wakes up, then breaks off when he notices something behind him.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED BASE/BARRACKS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Ventress orders Radek to freeze the video, revealing a blurred image of a deformed shirtless man with a twisted neck, missing jaw, and strangely elongated arms running toward the camera amid muzzle flash.

scenes.heading: INT. SCREEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The video continues showing Kane shooting the deformed man, then cuts to the cameraman running and taking cover with Kane as they exchange gunfire with unseen attackers.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED BASE/BARRACKS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Radek realizes with horror that the firefight on the video is taking place in the same barracks room where they're currently standing.

scenes.heading: INT. SCREEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The video shows Shelley and the cameraman finding Kane and Peyton in the shower room with Mayer handcuffed to a water pipe, arguing about whether to cut him open.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED BASE/BARRACKS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The five women stare at the black screen after the video ends, with Lena in a state beyond horror while the others process what they witnessed.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED BASE/BARRACKS/SHOWER ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena enters the shower room to find Mayer's desiccated body still cuffed to the chair with his ribs splayed open and skull placed at his feet.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE/BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena dreams of waking up in her bedroom where Kane brings her orange juice and tells her he's leaving for his mission a day early, pulling away when she tries to touch him.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED BASE/REC ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena wakes up in the abandoned base's rec room and sees Sheppard is also awake while Thorensen and Radek sleep nearby.
scenes.turningPointNote: Transition from dream to reality establishes Lena's psychological state and connection to Kane

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED BASE/REC ROOM HUT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena exits the rec room hut and finds Dr. Ventress studying a map by torchlight, who shows her their position and suggests heading to Ville Perdu tomorrow instead of the lighthouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SHIMMER - DAY
scenes.summary: Daybreak appears through the Shimmer.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED BASE/REC ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Radek sits shaking uncontrollably while arguing with Dr. Ventress about returning home, with Thorensen supporting Radek's position that they should leave after losing Sheppard and witnessing the previous team's violence.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED BASE/REC ROOM HUT - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena exits the hut with her gear and tells Dr. Ventress they're coming with her, while Dr. Ventress cryptically mentions that Lena's secret is safe with her.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: The four-person team walks through the swamp where Lena notices Thorensen muttering to herself and displaying a flash of anger and non-recognition when questioned, before Dr. Ventress stops upon finding a blood trail.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena follows the blood trail through the swamp with her gun raised, hearing noises and seeing movement that turns out to be a deer-like creature with branch and leaf structures growing from its back.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP/CLEARING - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena crawls through dense foliage and discovers Sheppard's body lying in a clearing, soaked with blood.

scenes.heading: INT. SWAMP/CLEARING - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena approaches Sheppard's mutilated body and witnesses her left eye make a sudden jittering movement and her lips part slightly, indicating she may still be alive.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena returns to the others and simply tells them that Sheppard is dead, with her finality discouraging further questions.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: The small group moves through the green landscape as distant figures before disappearing from view.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SUN - SUNDOWN
scenes.summary: The setting sun refracts into a rainbow through the Shimmer.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE VILLE PERDU - SUNDOWN
scenes.summary: The four women arrive at Ville Perdu, a small abandoned town with buildings half-swallowed by extreme moss tumor-growths, where Dr. Ventress decides they'll camp for the night.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLE PERDU - SUNDOWN
scenes.summary: The women enter the town with weapons ready and Radek spots what appears to be a man's silhouette between two buildings, causing everyone to freeze.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLE PERDU/STATUE GARDEN - SUNDOWN
scenes.summary: Lena leads the others past the man-shaped figure into a statue garden filled with twenty plant figures that have grown into human forms, with Lena determining they grew naturally rather than being carved.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLE PERDU/HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The women barricade themselves inside an abandoned house with Lena and Thorensen positioning furniture to block the front door before Lena volunteers for first watch.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOON - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The moon appears in the sky through the Shimmer.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLE PERDU/HOUSE/FRONT ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena sits watch in the house and after confirming the others are asleep, removes medical equipment from her backpack and uses a scalpel to cut her own forearm.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLE PERDU/HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena examines her blood under a microscope and sees something shimmering inside her blood cells, then walks past the sleeping figures to the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLE PERDU/HOUSE/KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena quietly vomits in the kitchen sink and when discovered by Radek, confirms that Radek's thesis is correct and that the Shimmer's influence is inside her.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY - DAY
scenes.summary: The moon appears in daylight sky.

scenes.heading: INT. JOHNS HOPKINS MEDICAL SCHOOL/SEMINAR ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena teaches her students about cancer as genetic mutation that causes unregulated cell growth, explaining that it frightens people because it changes them.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLE PERDU/STATUE GARDEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The moonlit human-shaped plant figures in the statue garden move gently in the wind.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE/BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena's face is shown in moonlight while having sex with someone.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE/BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena sits at her bedroom window after having sex with her colleague Daniel, calling it a mistake while he argues about their ongoing affair and her troubled marriage to Kane.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLE PERDU/HOUSE/FRONT ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena wakes to find Thorensen pointing a rifle at her head while Radek looks terrified and Dr. Ventress is missing, with Radek tossing Lena's locket containing Kane's photo onto her lap.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lena's secret relationship to Kane is exposed, fracturing the team

scenes.heading: INT. VILLE PERDU/HOUSE/FRONT ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Radek films with a camera as Thorensen interrogates Lena and Dr. Ventress, who are both tied to chairs, with Thorensen accusing them of working together in a setup while threatening them with her gun.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLE PERDU - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: First light appears over the town.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLE PERDU/HOUSE/PORCH - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Radek sits wounded and bandaged on the porch while Lena gives her water, with Radek learning that Dr. Ventress left for the lighthouse and asking why Lena didn't abandon her despite her fatal injuries.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLE PERDU/STATUE GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: A single gunshot echoes through the statue garden before Lena appears and sets all the plant figures on fire while howling with rage and grief.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lena mercy-kills Radek and burns the garden, marking her complete isolation

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena walks alone along a massive empty beach dotted with strange sand structures toward the distant white lighthouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE OCEAN - DAY
scenes.summary: A single figure walks along the shore between abstract sand structures while a whale-like creature surfaces and disappears in the ocean.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH/LIGHTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena approaches the lighthouse through an area littered with bones and clothing from an old massacre, picking up a skull that has fused with its own backbone.

scenes.heading: EXT. LIGHTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena approaches the lighthouse from below while its door hangs open, swinging in the sea breeze.

scenes.heading: INT. LIGHTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena enters the lighthouse base and finds military gear scattered on the floor along with a tunnel entrance in the floor with wooden boards arranged as a ramp leading downward.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lena reaches the lighthouse and discovers the tunnel entrance to the source

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: Camera playback shows Shelley screaming and struggling bare-chested while Peyton tries to hold him and Kane points a rifle at them, before Shelley breaks away and Kane shoots him.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: The camera shows Shelley lying shot in the grass with Kane and Peyton standing over him, revealing his strange deformations including a sideways mouth-like opening on his neck with something moving inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: Kane and Peyton stand on a beach watching a whale-sized dolphin launch itself from the ocean into the air.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Peyton fires his machine gun at the night sky while Kane tells him to stop wasting rounds, with Peyton claiming he's killing something that came from the stars.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH/LIGHTHOUSE
scenes.summary: The cameraman records an area of bones in the dunes near the lighthouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH/LIGHTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Peyton has a seizure-like episode that deforms his skull while Kane watches, then Kane shoots Peyton.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: Kane stands waist-deep in the ocean, pounding the water with his fists and screaming before staring back at the cameraman.

scenes.heading: EXT. LIGHTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Kane walks toward the lighthouse through towering sand structures.

scenes.heading: INT. LIGHTHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kane enters the lighthouse, removes his backpack and lays down his rifle.

scenes.heading: INT. LIGHTHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kane sits cross-legged in the lighthouse questioning his identity and reality before revealing a phosphorous grenade in his palm while speaking to the camera.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kane's existential breakdown and implied suicide with the grenade represents the climax of his psychological dissolution

scenes.heading: INT. LIGHTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena stares at Kane's frozen face on the camera screen and realizes the blackened heap is his remains, then hears a distorted female scream coming from a tunnel beneath torn floorboards.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDER THE LIGHTHOUSE/TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena descends into a phosphorescent tunnel with smooth black walls that appear to be moving and undulating.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDER THE LIGHTHOUSE/TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: As Lena continues down the tunnel, the rippled walls reveal embedded organic forms progressing from plants to animals to human shapes trying to break free.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDER THE LIGHTHOUSE/CHAMBER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena enters a chamber where Dr. Ventress stands glowing with cilia covering her skin, and Ventress transforms into a shimmering humanoid duplicate of Lena before the original Ventress disintegrates.
scenes.turningPointNote: The creation of Lena's duplicate represents the Shimmer's ultimate replication process and sets up the final confrontation

scenes.heading: INT. UNDER THE LIGHTHOUSE/TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena flees up the tunnel pursued by the humanoid, which mirrors her exact movements including stumbling and falling in the same places.

scenes.heading: INT. LIGHTHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The humanoid appears in front of Lena in the lighthouse and mirrors her attacks with greater force, sending her flying across the room twice before she ignites a phosphorous grenade.

scenes.heading: INT. LIGHTHOUSE/TOP - DAY
scenes.summary: Lena reaches the lighthouse top as the building burns below, confronted by the burned and damaged humanoid that continues transforming and mimicking her movements.

scenes.heading: EXT. LIGHTHOUSE - SUNDOWN
scenes.summary: The lighthouse burns as the Shimmer's colors settle and fade, sand structures collapse across the beach, and a figure walks across the sand.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Shimmer's dissolution marks the resolution of the alien threat and Lena's apparent escape

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/VIEWING PLATFORM - SUNDOWN
scenes.summary: People at the Southern Reach Facility gather on the viewing platform to watch the Shimmer melt away and revert to a normal sunset.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE NIGHT SKY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena walks out of the tree line toward the Southern Reach Facility under a starry night sky, requesting to see her husband.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/MEDICAL WARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A doctor escorts Lena through the medical ward, explaining that Kane recovered when the Shimmer disappeared and shows no abnormalities, as Lena requests to see him alone.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTHERN REACH FACILITY/WARD ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lena and Kane face each other in the ward room, with Kane admitting he doesn't know if he's really Kane and asking if she's really Lena as shooting stars appear outside the window.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mutual uncertainty about their identities reveals that both may be duplicates, fundamentally changing the meaning of their reunion

scenes.heading: INT. LOWER ATMOSPHERE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The shooting stars are revealed to be falling meteors with shimmering cores similar to the opening images, suggesting the cycle continues.
scenes.turningPointNote: The meteors' shimmering cores suggest the alien force is spreading beyond Earth, expanding the scope of the threat
